messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "oem-cell-two-sided-squeeze",
 "title": "The OEM/integrator cell is squeezed shut from both sides: brand-spec deals go to incumbent ecosystems, white-label seats go to scale ODMs",
 "narrative": "The 0.22 OEM/integrator cell looks like the natural entry channel for an industrial entrant — and superficially the evidence agrees, because BOTH routes into it are documented: integrators buy branded PDUs, and buyer's guides advertise OEM/private-label options. The trap is that each route is closed for a different reason. Every integrator case study found shows selection of branded incumbents on certification, DCIM compatibility and support ecosystems — assets a Year-1 entrant does not have. The white-label seats, meanwhile, are held by Asian ODMs (the Digipower pattern) competing on a manufacturing cost curve a Central European industrial entrant cannot match. The cell's 0.22 value is an unsourced internal estimate with an addressability premise no source supports; for this venture the genuinely open fraction may be a third of the modeled cell.",
 "category": "competitive",
 "targetNodes": [
  "cust.oem"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "Rack integrators select PDU brands on features, certification and DCIM-ecosystem compatibility — the documented integrator behavior favors incumbents whose monitoring stacks are already deployed — while the private-label demand that does exist is served by dedicated OEM/ODM manufacturers with global scale",
   "refs": [
    "digest:cust-mix#c14",
    "digest:cust-mix#c13",
    "ledger:cust.oem"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "An industrial entrant sits in the dead zone between the two: no installed DCIM/support ecosystem to win brand-spec designs (those accrue to the Legrand/Raritan/Server Technology axis and the top-5 vendor set) and no ODM cost position to win price-spec white-label seats",
   "refs": [
    "digest:cr3-vendors#c4",
    "digest:cr3-vendors#c1",
    "profile:Company"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "Addressable share of cust.oem falls from 0.22 to ~0.08 (the fraction of integrator demand that is regional, spec-flexible, and not price-anchored to ODM quotes); the funnel's channel mix shifts further onto cells that are themselves contested",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:cust.oem",
    "ledger:cust.operator-large"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "The cell appears doubly open — 'we can sell branded OR white-label' — which is exactly why it survives founder scrutiny. Two documented routes in feels like redundancy; it is actually two separately-owned gates, and the evidence that each gate is held (integrator brand loyalty; ODM incumbency) sits in different source types that never appear on the same page of a risk review.",
 "expectedObviousness": "low",
 "falsifier": "One CE rack integrator or OEM signing a design-in or white-label agreement with the entrant, or a documented integrator RFQ in which a non-incumbent, non-ODM intelligent PDU is evaluated on merit within the launch year.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.4,
  "rationale": "Every retrieved integrator case study shows incumbent-brand selection and no source quantifies an open OEM share, but the sample of case studies is small and integrator behavior for differentiated intelligent-PDU features is untested — judgment on how far the pattern generalizes.",
  "basis": "judgment"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "cust.oem",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.08,
   "note": "Only the regional, spec-flexible integrator fraction remains addressable; brand-spec and ODM-price fractions foreclosed"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "PDU brands named in European integrator/turnkey rack case studies and reference designs",
   "where": "Vertiv, Legrand, Rittal and Schleifenbauer project case-study pages; DataCenterDynamics build coverage",
   "threshold": "≥3 consecutive quarters of new CE integrator projects specifying only incumbent-brand PDUs",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "ODM intelligent-PDU pricing and OEM-program activity",
   "where": "Digipower and comparable ODM price lists / OEM program pages; Computex and Data Centre World exhibitor announcements",
   "threshold": "ODM per-outlet-managed PDU quotes ≥30% below the entrant's achievable unit cost",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Run a design-in probe with 3 CE rack integrators: submit the product to their qualification process and log the actual selection criteria and thresholds",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "cust.oem",
   "note": "Replaces the unsourced 0.22 addressability premise with observed gate criteria"
  },
  {
   "action": "Differentiate on the one axis neither incumbents nor ODMs own regionally: MID-compliant billing-grade metering and local engineering support for CE colo sub-metering requirements",
   "type": "strategic",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "A regulatory-technical feature can reopen brand-spec gates that price and ecosystem cannot"
  },
  {
   "action": "Offer integrators a co-branded (not white-label) program with shared support obligations, splitting the difference between the two closed routes",
   "type": "contractual",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": null
  }
 ],
 "lens": "competitive-foreclosure",
 "riskId": "risk.competitive-foreclosure.oem-cell-two-sided-squeeze"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).

The white-label seats, meanwhile, are held by Asian ODMs (the Digipower pattern) competing on a manufacturing cost curve a Central European industrial entrant cannot match. brand-spec and ODM-price fractions foreclosed"}],"indicators":[{"signal":"PDU brands named in European integrator/turnkey rack case studies and reference designs","where":"Vertiv, Legrand, Rittal and Schleifenbauer project case-study pages; DataCenterDynamics build coverage","threshold":"≥3 consecutive quarters of new CE integrator projects specifying only incumbent-brand PDUs","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"ODM intelligent-PDU pricing and OEM-program activity","where":"Digipower and comparable ODM price lists / OEM program pages; Computex and Data Centre World exhibitor announcements","threshold":"ODM per-outlet-managed PDU quotes ≥30% below the entrant's achievable unit cost","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Run a design-in probe with 3 CE rack integrators: submit the product to their qualification process and log the actual selection criteria and thresholds","type":"information","voiNodeId":"cust.oem","note":"Replaces the unsourced 0.22 addressability premise with observed gate criteria"},{"action":"Differentiate on the one axis neither incumbents nor ODMs own regionally: MID-compliant billing-grade metering and local engineering support for CE colo sub-metering requirements","type":"strategic","voiNodeId":null,"note":"A regulatory-technical feature can reopen brand-spec gates that price and ecosystem cannot"},{"action":"Offer integrators a co-branded (not white-label) program with shared support obligations, splitting the difference between the two closed routes","type":"contractual","voiNodeId":null,"note":null}]}
